articles.title: All the evidence revealed so far in Musk v. Altman
articles.summary: The Musk v. Altman trial reveals early OpenAI documents showing Musk drafted the company's mission and influenced its structure, while Nvidia provided crucial computing resources. Altman sought Y Combinator support as leadership expressed concerns about Musk's involvement.

articles.title: Pentagon strikes classified AI deals with OpenAI, Google, and Nvidia — but not Anthropic
articles.summary: The Pentagon signed classified AI deals with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, xAI, and Reflection, but excluded Anthropic citing supply-chain risks.

articles.title: Elon Musk had a bad week in court
articles.summary: Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming the company stole a nonprofit, appears unlikely to succeed. His courtroom testimony reportedly did not strengthen his case despite initiating the trial himself.

articles.title: Replit’s Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why he’d rather not sell
articles.summary: Replit's Amjad Masad discussed the company's future amid Cursor's potential $60 billion SpaceX acquisition, addressing whether Replit would also need to sell despite preferring independence.

articles.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
articles.summary: Meta acquired humanoid robotics startup Assured Robot Intelligence to strengthen its AI capabilities for robotic systems and advance its humanoid AI development goals.

articles.title: Did you know you can’t steal a charity? Don’t worry. Elon Musk will remind you.
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming Sam Altman betrayed the nonprofit mission by converting it to a for-profit model. Evidence including emails and tweets surfaced during the multi-day court proceedings.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud, capitalizing on developer frustration with legacy platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, but free open-source alternative Goose offers similar functionality locally without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt successfully attracted top engineering talent in a competitive hiring market.

articles.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
articles.summary: Ubuntu and Canonical servers have been offline for over 24 hours due to a sustained cross-border cyberattack, preventing OS updates and communications following a major vulnerability disclosure.

articles.title: The most severe Linux threat to surface in years catches the world flat-footed
articles.summary: A critical Linux vulnerability granting root access to virtually all releases was publicly exploited after researchers disclosed it to the kernel team. Most Linux distributions had not patched the flaw when exploit code was released.

articles.title: Why a recent supply-chain attack singled out security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden
articles.summary: Security firm Checkmarx suffered multiple supply-chain attacks within six weeks, including a Trivy vulnerability scanner breach and a direct GitHub compromise that delivered malware to customers. The firm was both a victim and unwitting delivery mechanism for attacks seeking credentials.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming CEO Sam Altman deceived him. He warned AI poses existential risks and admitted his xAI company uses OpenAI's models for training.

articles.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
articles.summary: AI is expanding cybersecurity attack surfaces and adding complexity, rendering legacy security approaches obsolete. Security must be fundamentally rethought with AI at its core rather than applied afterward.

articles.title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
articles.summary: Companies are taking control of their data to build customized AI systems while balancing data ownership with safe, high-quality data flow. AI factories enable organizations to achieve scale, sustainability, and governance while positioning data control as a strategic priority.

articles.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
articles.summary: AI evaluations are emerging as a critical bottleneck in AI development, rivaling computational resources as a limiting factor. Organizations must invest heavily in evaluation infrastructure to validate model performance effectively.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs represent an advanced approach to language model architecture, focusing on efficient training methods and improved performance across various tasks.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra joins Hugging Face as an inference provider, enabling developers to access and deploy machine learning models more easily through the Hugging Face ecosystem.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its evolving applications. The conversation explores upcoming developments and trends shaping the next phase of artificial intelligence.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and business operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for future farming technologies, enabling agricultural innovation through accessible tools and resources for farmers and developers.

articles.title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
articles.summary: Google Translate celebrates its 20th anniversary, evolving from a 2006 AI experiment to supporting nearly 250 languages. The company shared fun facts marking the milestone achievement.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.summary: Google and Kaggle are offering a 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with registration now open. The program aims to teach participants about building AI agents.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Gemini offers AI-powered tips for organizing your home and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox decluttering, and seasonal chore management.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. This new model aims to enhance clinical care through human-AI collaboration.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with the Republic of Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using frontier AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo enables resilient distributed AI training by allowing independent local updates across nodes before synchronization, improving fault tolerance and efficiency in large-scale model training.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #242 discusses major AI developments including OpenAI's new ChatGPT image model with improved text rendering, Qwen 3.6 Max, and Kimi-K2.6. The episode covers recent industry news and advances in AI capabilities.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. The episode discusses these releases alongside Mamba 3 and attention residuals developments in AI.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance real-time video game graphics with photorealism. The technology shows potential applications beyond gaming.

articles.title: SAP: How enterprise AI governance secures profit margins
articles.summary: SAP emphasizes that enterprise AI governance ensures profit margins by prioritizing precision and control over statistical approximations. Organizations deploying LLMs must shift focus from passive tools to active governance mechanisms for measurable business impact.

articles.title: Per-token AI charges come to GitHub Copilot
articles.summary: GitHub Copilot switches from flat-rate subscriptions to per-token pricing starting June 1st, 2026, aligning with standard LLM API billing models where users pay based on actual token consumption.

articles.title: What LG and NVIDIA’s talks reveal about the future of physical AI
articles.summary: LG and NVIDIA are exploring partnerships in physical AI, data centers, and mobility, revealing the massive infrastructure investments needed to deploy autonomous systems beyond simulation.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed automated methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges like localization and path planning in complex indoor environments, advancing general-purpose mobile robotics.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code accidentally leaks sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries, creating significant security risks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, creating significant supply chain security risks.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but undermines their ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems, revealing a tradeoff between faster reasoning and weaker generalization.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation to Parsing, Analyzing, Visualizing, and Fine-Tuning Agent Reasoning Traces Using the lambda/hermes-agent-reasoning-traces Dataset
articles.summary: This tutorial explores the lambda/hermes-agent-reasoning-traces dataset to understand agent behavior by parsing reasoning traces, analyzing tool usage patterns, creating visualizations, and preparing data for fine-tuning language models.

articles.title: A New NVIDIA Research Shows Speculative Decoding in NeMo RL Achieves 1.8× Rollout Generation Speedup at 8B and Projects 2.5× End-to-End Speedup at 235B
articles.summary: NVIDIA researchers integrated speculative decoding into NeMo RL to accelerate rollout generation, achieving 1.8× speedup at 8B and projecting 2.5× speedup at 235B while preserving model output distribution.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation of End-to-End Brain Decoding from MEG Signals Using NeuralSet and Deep Learning for Predicting Linguistic Features
articles.summary: Researchers developed an end-to-end brain decoding system using MEG signals and deep learning to predict linguistic features like word length from neural activity. The pipeline leverages neuroAI tools, NeuralSet, and convolutional neural networks to extract meaningful patterns from brain signals.

articles.title: T-Mobile is giving 5G customers Hulu and Netflix subscriptions, free - here's how to get them
articles.summary: T-Mobile customers on Experience Beyond or Go5G Next plans now receive free Hulu and Netflix subscriptions with ads included in their service.

articles.title: 5 last-minute gifts your mom will actually like for Mother's Day (and all are on sale)
articles.summary: Last-minute Mother's Day gift guide featuring useful gadgets and cozy items approved by staff members' mothers. All recommended products are currently on sale.

articles.title: Samsung will give you a free 32-inch Odyssey monitor right now - how to qualify
articles.summary: Samsung is offering a buy-one-get-one deal on monitors, giving customers a free 32-inch Odyssey monitor with qualifying purchases. Check Samsung's terms for eligibility details.

articles.title: Effective KV Compression with TurboQuant
articles.summary: Google launched TurboQuant, a novel algorithmic suite for applying advanced quantization and compression to large language models and vector search engines, essential for RAG systems.

articles.title: Building AI Agents in Python with Pydantic AI
articles.summary: Pydantic AI enables developers to build AI agents in Python with structured validation. The framework simplifies agent development through type-safe patterns and automated data handling.

articles.title: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Developer’s Guide
articles.summary: Guide on context engineering techniques for AI agents, helping developers optimize prompt design and information structuring to improve agent performance and decision-making capabilities.

articles.title: How a 2021 Quantization Algorithm Quietly Outperforms Its 2026 Successor
articles.summary: A 2021 quantization algorithm unexpectedly outperforms its newer 2026 successor, with a single scale parameter being crucial to rotation-based vector quantization accuracy.

articles.title: How to Get Hired in the AI Era
articles.summary: The article explores what hiring managers seek in junior candidates during the AI era, highlighting qualities that help candidates stand out in competitive job markets.

articles.title: Churn Without Fragmentation: How a Party-Label Bug Reversed My Headline Finding
articles.summary: A data quality case study reveals how a party-label bug in English local election analysis reversed initial findings about voter volatility, highlighting the importance of categorical normalization and metric validation.

articles.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
articles.summary: OpenClaw agents have become a significant open-source phenomenon, reaching 100,000 GitHub stars by early 2026. The project enables organizations to build specialized AI systems using open models and datasets on NVIDIA platforms.

articles.title: It’s Gonna Be May: 16 Games Hit the Cloud This Month, With More NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Power
articles.summary: 16 games launch on GeForce NOW in May, including Forza Horizon 6 and 007 First Light. NVIDIA upgrades RTX 5080 power for cloud gaming across multiple platforms.

articles.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Model, Unifying Vision, Audio and Language for up to 9x More Efficient AI Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a unified multimodal model combining vision, audio, and language capabilities into one system, enabling 9x more efficient AI agents with faster responses and advanced reasoning.

articles.title: AWS Transform now automates BI migration to Amazon Quick in days
articles.summary: AWS Transform automates migration of BI dashboards to Amazon QuickSight, reducing timelines from months to days while preserving domain knowledge and analytics capabilities.

articles.title: Reinforcement fine-tuning with LLM-as-a-judge
articles.summary: Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RFT) using automated reward signals improves LLM alignment and accuracy. LLM-as-a-judge and verifiable rewards replace costly manual labeling for domain-specific model training.

articles.title: AWS Generative AI Model Agility Solution: A comprehensive guide to migrating LLMs for generative AI production
articles.summary: AWS provides a comprehensive guide for migrating large language models in production, enabling organizations to maintain model agility while optimizing AI solutions. The solution requires a structured, automated approach that balances generality with specificity across diverse use cases.

articles.title: Speed Up Unreal Engine NNE Inference with NVIDIA TensorRT for RTX Runtime
articles.summary: NVIDIA TensorRT accelerates neural network inference in Unreal Engine's NNE runtime for RTX GPUs, enabling faster image quality improvements and real-time graphics enhancements like super resolution and denoising.

articles.title: Build AI-Powered Games with NVIDIA DLSS 4.5, RTX, and Unreal Engine 5
articles.summary: NVIDIA releases DLSS 4.5 with Dynamic Multi Frame Generation and enhanced Super Resolution for game developers using Unreal Engine 5, enabling AI-powered graphics improvements.

articles.title: How to Build, Run, and Scale High-Quality Creator Workflows in ComfyUI
articles.summary: ComfyUI is an open-source tool that helps creative teams automate asset production using generative AI, compressing manual tasks into efficient, repeatable workflows.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study by CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational attitudes toward digital advertising.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these innovations openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background developed innovative extended reality technology by applying unique perspectives and skills outside traditional XR development paths.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for learned world models that enables practical long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient signals for action selection.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to identify and understand interactions at scale within Large Language Models to improve interpretability and AI transparency. This work aims to make LLM decision-making processes more transparent for safer, more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven design methods for imaging systems that optimize optical encoders to create distinguishable measurements despite noise corruption, using only noisy data and noise models.
